title: crassula atropurpurea var. cultriformis
intro: Crassula atropurpurea var. cultriformis, also known as the 'Red Pagoda', is a succulent plant species in the family Crassulaceae, characterized by its red-tinged, upright leaves and stems. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Crassula atropurpurea var. cultriformis include treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating digestive issues, as well as providing antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems can be used to make teas, infusions, and salves, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
